content.method: Capacity-simulation modeling using current teacher training enrollment, retirement projections, and cohort-size forecasts to stress-test proposed multi-year phase-in schedules against demographic scenarios; comparative process-tracing of Poland's 2017 implementation timeline versus proposed Hungarian phasing design.
content.question: What would be the transition costs (capacity shortfalls, unplaced students, teacher redeployment strain) of phasing in a delayed tracking age given Hungary's current teacher workforce shortage and aging demographic pyramid, and can phased sequencing avoid Poland's 2017 reversal shock?
content.why_it_matters: T3 is the highest-friction, highest-potential-impact proposal; its feasibility hinges on whether Hungary's already-strained teacher supply and irregular cohort sizes make a Polish-style disruption likely despite phasing safeguards.
